section.heading: Priority placement, job placement, retraining, and counseling programs for United States Department of Energy employees affected by reduction in force

paragraph.content: For the purposes of this section, the term “agency” means the United States Department of Energy.

paragraph.chapeau: For the purposes of this section, the term “eligible employee” means any employee of the agency who—

subparagraph.chapeau: is scheduled to be separated from service due to a reduction in force under—

clause.content: regulations prescribed under section 3502 of title 5; or
procedures established under section 3595 of title 5; or

subparagraph.chapeau: is separated from service due to such a reduction in force, but does not include—

clause.content: an employee separated from service for cause on charges of misconduct or delinquency; or
an employee who, at the time of separation, meets the age and service requirements for an immediate annuity under subchapter III of chapter 83 or chapter 84 of title 5.

content.p: Not later than 30 days after September 30, 1996, the United States Department of Energy shall establish an agency-wide priority placement and retraining program for eligible employees.

subsection.heading: Filling vacancy from outside agency
subsection.chapeau: The priority placement program established under subsection (b) shall include provisions under which a vacant position shall not be filled by the appointment or transfer of any individual from outside of the agency if—

paragraph.content: there is then available any eligible employee who applies for the position within 30 days of the agency issuing a job announcement and is qualified (or can be trained or retrained to become qualified within 90 days of assuming the position) for the position; and
the position is within the same commuting area as the eligible employee’s last-held position or residence.

subsection.heading: Job placement and counseling services
subsection.chapeau: The head of the agency may establish a program to provide job placement and counseling services to eligible employees. A program established under subsection (d) may include, but is not limited to, such services as—

paragraph.content: training and job search skills; and
job placement assistance, including assistance provided through cooperative arrangements with State and local employment services offices.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was enacted as part of the Energy and Water Development Appropriations Act, 1997, and not as part of the Department of Energy Organization Act which comprises this chapter.
